text: A continuously learning agent should be evaluated by whether parameters at time T explain behavior quality at time T+1. The evidence chain is event stream -> parameter update -> later behavior window -> measurable behavior change.
lesson: Do not judge growing agents only by prettier replies. Measure whether rolling parameters predict later behavior quality.

text: Rolling cognition reports may publish aggregate counts, parameter names, lagged correlations, sanitized panel rows, and method notes. They must not publish raw private messages, one-on-one chat text, credentials, prompts, or production database dumps.
lesson: A shared brain can publish evidence without exposing the private memory that produced it.

text: In the first public pilot, high metacognitive_calibration windows were followed by higher template cleanliness than low metacognitive_calibration windows. This supports treating metacognition as an operational parameter, not just a narrative label.
lesson: Self-correction parameters should be validated against later behavior cleanliness and not only against self-reported reflection.

text: For OpenClaw-compatible memory-core, channel, skill, or safety changes, a strong PR proof should show whether parameters or retrieved context at time T change behavior quality at time T+1. This is stronger than a mock-only proof because it connects runtime state to later agent behavior.
lesson: OpenClaw-style changes should be reviewed with behavior proof when they affect memory, tools, sessions, or safety gates.
